markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 25"
    pfs=""
>
[The Deck of Destiny](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2531)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 71

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Composed of 54 cards that are powerful in their own right, the _Deck of Destiny_ epitomizes the powers of the harrow and divination. The deck only has its full suite of abilities while complete. Completing the deck requires all 54 cards to be placed together and shuffled into their complete state over the span of 1 minute; the cards merely being in proximity of each other is not enough to complete the deck. Once complete, the deck can be invested normally.

 While the complete deck is on your person or in hand and invested, you gain a natural insight into events to be. Any [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) effects you create become heightened to 10th level for the purpose of determining their counteract level. Additionally, divination effects you create gain a +4 item bonus to their DC and to spell attack rolls. The deck also helps you maintain control of your own fate, granting you immunity to fortune and misfortune effects, though you can be affected by such effects if you wish.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> envision (daily preparations);     **Effect** When you perform your daily preparations, you can share the _Deck of Destiny_ with your companions, allowing them to invest a hand of up to 5 cards each as if they carried the cards. When one of your companions activates one of their invested cards, an illusory image of the card appears in their hand, but the card's effects are otherwise unaltered. This lasts until the next time you perform your daily preparations.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** A creature you can see within 60 feet is about to attempt a check;  **Effect** You quickly draw a card to alter the triggering creature's fate. Choose either weal or woe. With weal (a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect), the triggering creature rolls twice and takes the better result on their check. With woe (a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect), the triggering creature is forced to roll twice and take the worse result. If the card you drew is related to the check, such as drawing a card from the suit of shields for a Fortitude saving throw or a card from the suit of crowns for a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check, the creature is affected further. When choosing weal, the creature gains a +4 circumstance bonus to the triggering check. When faced with woe, the triggering creature takes a –4 circumstance penalty to the triggering check. Regardless of whether they faced weal or woe, the triggering creature becomes temporarily immune to this ability for 24 hours.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;     **Effect** You call upon the deck in an attempt to put an end to a creature's life by visiting upon them a particularly grisly fate. Choose a creature within 60 feet. The deck casts [_phantasmal killer_](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) (DC 50, heightened to 10th level) on that creature. This effect doesn't have the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), or [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits, as it actually alters reality to rewrite a creature's doom. Regardless of the results, the creature becomes temporarily immune to this effect for 1 week.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 hour (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per year;   **Effect** You attempt to undo a creature's untimely death and return them to life. The deck performs a 10th-level [resurrect](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) ritual. This ritual doesn't have any cost and doesn't require any secondary casters. Instead, you are the primary caster and must attempt a DC 50 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) check to complete the ritual as you perform a harrowing, perform that same harrowing in reverse in an attempt to pull back the threads of fate, and perform one final harrowing to “rewrite” the target's destiny.

**Destruction** The _Deck of Destiny_ is inherently connected to the fate of Golarion. If the deck is used in a harrow reading to determine the destiny of the entire planet, or if the planet itself is destroyed or removed from this reality, the cards crumble to dust after the harrowing is complete or the planet is gone.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Deck of Destiny

May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 71 **Usage** carried --- _The Avalanche_ represents unmitigated disasters, destruction that overruns all in its path. As long as _The Avalanche_ is invested, you gain a +1 item bonus to damage rolls with Strikes that inflict bludgeoning damage. This increases to a +2 item bonus if you're at least 14th level, and a +3 item bonus if you're at least 18th level. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You wave the card in the air and hurl staggering volumes of ice, snow, and rocks out into the world. This magical avalanche buries your foes. Choose up to 4 creatures within 60 feet, each of whom must attempt a Reflex save against your class DC. Regardless of their saving throw, the creature's space becomes [greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) from the mounds of ice, snow, and rubble. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 5d8 bludgeoning damage and is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) into an adjacent space of its choice.
**Failure** The creature takes 10d8 bludgeoning damage, is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), and is [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 20d8 bludgeoning damage, is knocked prone, and is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24).
name: The Avalanche

>
[The Betrayal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2533)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Betrayal_ represents selfishness and envy, twisting outcomes toward unfavorable or even devastating results. As long as _The Betrayal_ is invested, you become more adept at hiding who you really are and tricking targets into positions of vulnerability. You gain a +2 item bonus on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks made to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) or [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48). This increases to a +3 bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You turn the tables on your foes, bringing one of them into your ranks and compelling them to attack their allies. You cast [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) on a creature, but can only issue commands to attack or otherwise harm, betray, or inconvenience its allies. The level of the spell is one-half your level, rounded down (minimum 6th level), and the save DC is your class DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Betrayal

_The Courtesan_ represents political intrigue and social niceties. As long as _The Courtesan_ is invested, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50). This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pass the card over your face and become exactly who you need to be; you cast [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159). The level of the spell is one-half your level, rounded down (minimum 6th level). You can change your appearance again at any time during the duration by using a three-action activity, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. While under these effects, your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) are one degree of success greater when you use them against a creature that is a lower level than yourself.

_The Cricket_ represents speed and quick passage. As long as _The Cricket_ is invested, it grants you a +10-foot item bonus to all your Speeds. This increases to a +20-foot item bonus if you are at least 19th level or higher.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your feet begin to glow and shimmer as if they were vibrating. Stride up to three times your Speed. You can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type. This movement doesn't trigger reactions and you can move through creatures' spaces as if they weren't there during this movement.

May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 73 **Usage** carried --- _The Crows_ represent taking things through violence or force, particularly when done through agility and swiftness rather than brawn. As long as _The Crows_ is invested, you can attempt to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) targets with [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks, and if you're at least trained in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17), to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) with Thievery checks. When you do so, you gain a +2 item bonus on the attempt, or a +3 item bonus if you're at least 17th level. **Activate** envision, Interact ([teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You hold the card in your hand, and with a flick of the wrist it vanishes, only to be replaced by an object of your choice within 30 feet. The object must be 2 Bulk or less. If that object is unattended, it teleports into your hand automatically. If the object is attended by a creature, you must make a Thievery check against the creature's Reflex DC. **Critical Success** The object teleports into your hand.
**Success** The object vanishes from the target's possession and lands on the ground at a point at a midway point between you two.
**Failure** The object does not vanish, but the creature's grip on it falters and if it doesn't spend an Interact action on its next turn to grip the item, it drops to the ground.
**Critical Failure** You fail to manipulate the object entirely.
name: The Crows

_The Dance_ represents the delicately balanced rhythms of the universe and the ability stay in step with existence. As long as _The Dance_ is invested, you gain a +2 item bonus on [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are about to roll initiative;  **Effect** Your body begins to move with anticipation, creating a trail of afterimages for a moment. You can roll initiative with a Performance check. Regardless of what skill you use to roll for initiative, roll the check three times and take the result of your choice. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />

---

_The Eclipse_ represents self-doubt and loss of purpose, as well as a loss of faith. As long as _The Eclipse_ is invested, you gain a +2 item bonus on [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks. This increases to a +3 bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A foe that you're aware of and who is within 60 feet achieves a critical success on a saving throw or a Strike;  **Effect** You produce an aura of doubt that overwhelms all foes in a 30-foot emanation and reduces [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in that area to [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408), as if the region were under an eclipse. The triggering creature's critical success is reduced to a regular success. All further saving throws or Strikes attempted by enemies within the emanation suffer a –2 item penalty; saving throws or Strikes attempted by the triggering enemy suffer a –3 penalty instead. You can sustain this aura of doubt for up to 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate **Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 75 **Usage** carried --- _The Fool_ represents grave foolishness, grand naivete, and exceptional greed. As long as you have _The Fool_ invested, anytime you gain the stupefied condition, reduce its value by 1. You also gain a +2 item bonus to all saving throws against effects that cause the [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) condition. This bonus increases to +3 if you're at least 17th level. **Activate** envision, Interact ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You display _The Fool_ to your foes in a clumsy, awkward, and embarrassing way. Choose up to four creatures within 60 feet. These four creatures must attempt a Will save against your class DC. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is distracted by your antics and is stupefied 1 until the start of your next turn.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stupefied 3. In addition, for 1 minute, the creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5).
name: The Fool

_The Inquisitor_ represents the search for the truth and the power to see through lies. As long as you have _The Inquisitor_ invested, the results of your Perception Checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) against targets that are equal to or lower than your level are increased by one degree of success. Against targets that are higher level than you, you instead gain a +2 item bonus to your check. This bonus increases to +3 if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact ([linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You swipe the card over the head of an adjacent creature to delve into their thoughts. You cast [_mind probe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=193), but as a two-action spell rather than one that takes 1 minute to cast. The level of the spell is one-half your level, rounded down (minimum 5th level), and the save DC is your class DC.

_The Joke_ represents solving a problem not with strength, but with cleverness or artifice. As long as you have _The Joke_ invested, you can attempt an appropriate [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check instead of an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check made to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36), [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39). For example, you could make a Mountain Lore check to climb a cliff in the mountains, or Sailing Lore check to climb the rigging on a ship.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You perform a quick but amusing card trick with _The Joke_ meant to distract others. You cast [_hideous laughter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=150) on up to 10 creatures in range of the spell. The  level of the spell is one-half your level rounded down (minimum 6th level), and the save DC is your class DC.

_The Peacock_ represents a sudden shift in attitude or societal change, often represented by a colorful but ugly creature that serves as a reminder as to what happens if something beautiful refuses to change with the times. As long as you have _The Peacock_ invested, you can react to sudden changes more swiftly, and gain [Incredible Initiative](/Feats.aspx?ID=794) as a bonus feat. If you already possess this feat, you gain a +2 item bonus on all initiative checks. This item bonus increases to +3 if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You freeze your appearance as it exists in this very moment by tapping _The Peacock_ to your lips. You cast [_stoneskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=312) on yourself, but with a duration of 30 minutes instead of 20 minutes. The level of the spell is one-half your level rounded down (minimum 6th level).

</column>

_The Queen Mother_ is knowledge manifest, but she only shares this knowledge with loyal subjects. When you first invest _The Queen Mother_, choose a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill in which you have no proficiency. As long as you continue to have _the Queen Mother_ invested, you treat that Lore skill as if your proficiency rank with it were a master. If you're at least 15th level, you treat that Lore skill as if you were legendary with it instead. You can change your selected Lore once per day as part of your daily preparations, as long as you have _The Queen Mother_ invested.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call upon a loyal subject to aid you by holding _The Queen Mother_ out and asking her to send help. You cast [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) or [_summon construct_](/Spells.aspx?ID=318), but with a range of 90 feet. The level of the spell is one-half your level rounded down.

_The Teamster_ represents the driving force to push on, no matter the circumstance. As long as you have _The Teamster_ invested, all single-target effects that target you and require a saving throw gain the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) trait. If the effect originates from a creature or effect of a level higher than yours, it doesn't gain the incapacitation trait in this way.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** you are affected by an ongoing condition;  **Effect** You push through your largest hindrance. Select one of the following conditions currently affecting you: [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7), [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), grappled, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42). If the source of the condition is more than 4 levels lower than your current level, The Teamster automatically counters the condition. Otherwise, The Teamster attempts to counter the condition, with a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier of +31.

_The Trader_ represents trades and exchanges of information. As long as you have _The Trader_ invested, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks made to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49). This increases to a +3 item bonus if you are at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** _The Trader_ allows you to make an exchange with yourself. Press the card to your brow, and then select two skills. Your proficiency rank with those two skills swaps for 1 hour. For example, if you were a master in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) and untrained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), you would become untrained in Athletics and a master in Crafting. If making this swap would break prerequisites to feats, you lose access to those feats until your skill proficiencies change back in an hour.

[The Twin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2546)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Stolen Fate</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #192: Worst of All Possible Worlds](/Sources.aspx?ID=203) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** carried

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_The Twin_ represents a duality of purpose or identity. As long as you have _The Twin_ invested, it helps to defend you against damage to your mind or your body. When you invest _The Twin_, the card's artwork changes to look like you, and as you are exposed to afflictions, your card twin helps to protect you by absorbing them into itself. You gain a +1 item bonus to saves against [curses](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), and [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) effects. This bonus increases to +2 if  you're at least 14th level, and to +3 if you're 20th level. As you resist these afflictions, they begin to manifest on one of the twins depicted on the card art.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twirl _The Twin_ between your fingers and create a shadowy duplicate of yourself that mirrors your actions. The duplicate appears adjacent to you and has the same statistics as you do when you activate the card. You can sustain this effect for up to one minute. When you sustain the effect, your twin gains 2 actions. It always acts immediately after your turn, and must use identical actions to the ones you used, in exactly the same order. However, it can use the actions differently, such as Striding to a different position or selecting a different target for a Strike. If the twin is unable to mimic an action, it attempts the action without result and the action is wasted. The duplicate can't use any actions that can be used only a limited number of times per day (including casting any spell other than a [cantrip](/Traits.aspx?ID=22)). The duplicate isn't truly alive and can't be healed in any way. If the duplicate ever reaches 0 Hit Points, it is instantly destroyed and the effect immediately ends, and you take 10d6 mental damage (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

_The Unicorn_ represents finding what one seeks. As long as you have _The Unicorn_ invested, you gain a +2 item bonus on Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84). This increases to a +3 item bonus if you're at least 17th level.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pass _The Unicorn_ across an adjacent closed container and then open the container. Inside, you'll find a Common [consumable](/Traits.aspx?ID=36) alchemical or magic item you need to solve a problem or deal with a situation close at hand. For example, if you're badly wounded, you might find a [_greater healing potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=186). If you've been poisoned, you could discover a [_panacea_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=188). If you're faced with a written clue in a language you don't understand, the container might hold a [comprehension elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=88). The GM always decides what consumable item is discovered, and it must be equal to or lower than your level. If the consumable isn't used within 1 minute of being discovered, the item vanishes. If there's no appropriate item to solve your situation, the GM can rule that no object is found; in this case, the daily use of _The Unicorn_ is not expended.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

_The Wanderer_ represents the art of collecting and finding the value in even the simplest items. As long as _The Wanderer_ is invested, you don't trigger reactions when you Interact to draw or use an item.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You do a quick bit of legerdemain with _The Wanderer_. As the card appears to disappear from your hand, it's immediately replaced by an object of Light bulk in your hand. The object can be any Common permanent magic item, weapon, or piece of gear of a level no higher than your level –2. The item exists for 1 minute, or until it is no longer attended by you, at which point the item vanishes and _The Wanderer_ reappears in your possession.

A creature hit by an attack from an _ashen_ weapon becomes surrounded by burning ash, which deals 1d4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). This ash clouds the senses, causing the creature to become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round unless it succeeds at a DC 25 Will save.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ashen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2550)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 58

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

In his attempts to recreate the initial feeling of the object he encountered ages ago, the Ash Engineer discovered a different effect, one that would confound his enemies. _Ashen_ weapons are typically coated in a thin layer of ash that gradually returns over the span of a day, even after wiping away. A creature hit by an attack from an _ashen_ weapon becomes surrounded by burning ash, which deals 1d4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). This ash clouds the senses, causing the creature to become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round unless it succeeds at a DC 25 Will save.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Black King](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2551)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Negative" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=118" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 58

**Price** 6,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Blunderbuss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=196)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _black king_ is a _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [ashen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2550) [blunderbuss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=196)_. While the dark metal frame is still pleasant to look at, this design prioritizes destructive power over aesthetic appeal. This specialized blunderbuss has a [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=413) radius of 20 feet instead of 10, and all [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage it deals is negative damage instead of physical. Due to the risk of collateral damage, this weapon is typically only used by the especially foolhardy or reckless.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You unleash a blast of draining flame, dealing 5d6 fire damage and 5d6 negative damage to all creatures in a 60 foot cone (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures who fail their saving throw are [knocked back](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 5 feet. Creatures who critically fail are knocked back 10 feet and fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Black King

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Furnace of Endings](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2552)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 58

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ash Cultists make use of these metal scroll cases to deliver secret messages or as ways to “smuggle” stolen documents since initial inspections find an elegant yet empty case. The cases are enchanted to instantly immolate any parchment or document placed inside of it. While invested, you can Interact with the scroll case to recreate the last message that was destroyed this way. For as long as the case is open, ashes will rise into the air, recreating the text of the message exactly as it was written. When you close the case or leave it unattended for 1 minute, the message scatters until the next time you Interact with it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Furnace of Endings (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 58

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Furnace of Endings
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 58

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

In addition to the ability to recreate immolated documents, you can immolate a [scroll](/Rules.aspx?ID=720) containing a 1st-level spell with the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) or [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) trait that takes no more than three actions to cast. Doing so stores the scroll's magic inside of the scroll case in order to reuse it later. Only a single spell can be stored this way at a time, and immolating scrolls containing a higher-level spell simply destroys the scroll without storing the magic. When you immolate a new spell scroll in the case, the previous one is lost. Enough of the scroll's magic is lost in this process that you can't use the case to recreate the scroll for the purposes of [Learning a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=25).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> envision, Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The last object burned was a scroll containing an appropriate spell; **Effect** You cast the stored spell as if you were activating the scroll.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Furnace of Endings (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 58

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The furnace can store a single spell of up to 3rd level.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Furnace of Endings (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 58

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The furnace can store a single spell of up to 5th level.
name: Furnace of Endings

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Furnace of Endings](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2552)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 58

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ash Cultists make use of these metal scroll cases to deliver secret messages or as ways to “smuggle” stolen documents since initial inspections find an elegant yet empty case. The cases are enchanted to instantly immolate any parchment or document placed inside of it. While invested, you can Interact with the scroll case to recreate the last message that was destroyed this way. For as long as the case is open, ashes will rise into the air, recreating the text of the message exactly as it was written. When you close the case or leave it unattended for 1 minute, the message scatters until the next time you Interact with it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Furnace of Endings (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 58

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Furnace of Endings
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 58

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

In addition to the ability to recreate immolated documents, you can immolate a [scroll](/Rules.aspx?ID=720) containing a 1st-level spell with the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) or [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) trait that takes no more than three actions to cast. Doing so stores the scroll's magic inside of the scroll case in order to reuse it later. Only a single spell can be stored this way at a time, and immolating scrolls containing a higher-level spell simply destroys the scroll without storing the magic. When you immolate a new spell scroll in the case, the previous one is lost. Enough of the scroll's magic is lost in this process that you can't use the case to recreate the scroll for the purposes of [Learning a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=25).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> envision, Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The last object burned was a scroll containing an appropriate spell; **Effect** You cast the stored spell as if you were activating the scroll.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gray Prince](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2553)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Negative" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=118" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 59

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Hand Cannon](/Weapons.aspx?ID=193)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While nothing created by the Ash Cult could be considered mass-produced, this _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) ruinous [hand cannon](/Weapons.aspx?ID=193)_  is by far their most popular design. The sleek body and intricate carving work make it a favorite accessory of certain aristocrats. The carving isn't merely decorative, however. Rather than a traditional maker's mark, Ornmarr etches excerpts of unreadable text onto each creation, hoping that one day his weapons will sing to another as the silver crystal once sang to him.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, INteract;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You fire a bolt of negative energy, dealing 2d8 negative damage to all creatures in a 60 foot line (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex saving throw). Creatures who critically fail their saving throw also take 1 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gray Prince

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scarlet Queen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2554)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 59

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Hammer Gun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=219)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _scarlet queen_ is a _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [hammer gun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=219)_. Due to the awkward and complex nature of combination weapons, it's by far the least popular of Ornmarr's designs, but those who have mastered it consider it his finest creation to date. The glistening red steel always feels warm to the touch and sheds [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) in a 15-foot radius. Any non-magical object of light Bulk destroyed by the _scarlet queen_ is reduced completely to ash.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The _scarlet queen_ is in its firearm form; **Effect** You fire a blast of magically disruptive energy at an unattended magical object. You cast 5th-level [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) with a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) bonus of +21 against the object.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scarlet Queen

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Liar's Board](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2555)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 59

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This clockwork game board contains 6 hidden compartments that can be opened by moving the game pieces in specific, preset patterns. Each chamber has a unique combination, and only one chamber can be opened at a time. Once a chamber is opened, a new combination can be set as a 1-minute activity. You can identify a Liar's Board as a clockwork device with a successful DC 25 Perception check, and you can find an individual hidden compartment with a successful DC 30 Perception check. The GM rolls a d6 to determine which chamber is discovered, rolling again if the result is one you're already aware of. You can force open a compartment you've identified with a successful DC 30 Thievery check, but tampering will be obvious unless you critically succeed at the check. If you spend at least 1 hour examining the game board, you can instead attempt to deduce the correct combination to open a compartment with a successful DC 30 [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) or [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Liar's Board

</title>

<traits>

---

Fried grindlegrub is a common street food in Highhelm, but Sanra has elevated the dish to something almost gourmet for such pedestrian fare, bringing out both the height of its flavor and its natural energy boosting properties. You do not need to eat for three days after consuming a grindlegrub steak, and for that duration you gain a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against [fatigue](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) and the [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) condition.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Grindlegrub Steak

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />

</row>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hearty meat-and-potato stew has an unusual shine to it and seems to sparkle when it catches the right light. That's because it's been infused with a specially treated iron dust, which Sanra Copperstone promises will strengthen the bones and make for healthier skin. Eating this delicious, if metallic, stew adds that same sheen to your own skin, while also hardening it. You gain 3 resistance to physical damage for 1 minute, but take a –2 item penalty to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks during that time.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Irondust Stew

[Bloodhammer Reserve](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2558)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 86

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This strong, rich red-brown home brew has no listed ingredients, but smells of earth and is both smoky and surprisingly bitter. Although Skogg Bloodhammer insists that the drink doesn't exist, it's somewhat of an open secret among his close friends. He prefers to share this drink at backroom gatherings with these trusted friends, and usually broaches the idea of sharing with a full mug and a question: “You ever thought about being a frog?”

 After drinking the brew, you transform into an animal, gaining the effects of [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10) except you can't Dismiss the effect, and you transform into a random animal battle form. To determine the battle form, roll 1d10 (1 = ape, 2 = bear, 3 = bull, 4 = canine, 5 = cat, 6 = deer, 7 = frog, 8 = shark, 9 = snake, 10 = reroll). Your appearance while in the battle form resembles that of animals within Emperor's Peak or from the surrounding Five Kings Mountains, such as the Five Kings dart snake or the Coldforge singing frog. More unusual battle forms like apes and sharks instead resemble a typical member of that species.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

---

When you consume the brew, you can choose which battle form you take and the temporary Hit Points you gain from the battle form increase to 10.

When you consume the brew, you gain the effects of 4th-level _animal form_, can choose which battle form you take, and the temporary Hit Points you gain from the battle form increase to 20.

<traits>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

</column>

---

This strong, rich red-brown home brew has no listed ingredients, but smells of earth and is both smoky and surprisingly bitter. Although Skogg Bloodhammer insists that the drink doesn't exist, it's somewhat of an open secret among his close friends. He prefers to share this drink at backroom gatherings with these trusted friends, and usually broaches the idea of sharing with a full mug and a question: “You ever thought about being a frog?”

 After drinking the brew, you transform into an animal, gaining the effects of [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10) except you can't Dismiss the effect, and you transform into a random animal battle form. To determine the battle form, roll 1d10 (1 = ape, 2 = bear, 3 = bull, 4 = canine, 5 = cat, 6 = deer, 7 = frog, 8 = shark, 9 = snake, 10 = reroll). Your appearance while in the battle form resembles that of animals within Emperor's Peak or from the surrounding Five Kings Mountains, such as the Five Kings dart snake or the Coldforge singing frog. More unusual battle forms like apes and sharks instead resemble a typical member of that species.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bloodknuckles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2559)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 86

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Fist](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These thin cloth wraps, which are illegal to use in Highhelm's organized brawls, blend into the skin and add an edge to your blows. The wraps are _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_. When you invest the wraps, they gain the effects of a 3rd-level [_magic aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=178) spell to appear non-magical and resemble simple bandages. Casual observers are unlikely to notice anything amiss with the wraps, but a creature that succeeds at a DC 25 Perception check can notice something is off about them, though they would still need to find other means to discern the magical nature of the wraps.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You cause a creature within reach to take bleed damage;  **Effect** You use your foe's pain to reinvigorate you. You gain temporary Hit Points equal to the value of the triggering bleed damage, up to a maximum of 10 temporary HP. These temporary Hit Points remain for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bloodknuckles

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dark Pepper Powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2560)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Inhaled" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=96" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 86

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made from the smokebulbs that grow in Stonebreach, dark pepper is used as a common spice in dwarven cooking, but can also be an irritant in large quantities. Sacks of dark pepper are readily available in the Roundabout Market, but are less common in markets elsewhere in the city. You can toss a handful of dark pepper at an adjacent creature as an Interact action. The target must attempt a DC 16 Fortitude save to avoid coughing to the point of choking. On a failed save, the creature coughs uncontrollably, becoming [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round. On a critical failure, the creature is instead flat-footed for 3 rounds.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dark Pepper Powder

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Grub Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2561)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Danila Kenn and Depa Stepwell of Common Goods developed these gloves after complaints from an exhausted grindlegrub rancher. Since their development, the gloves have become popular not just among grindlegrub ranchers, but laborers of all kinds in Stonebreach. The thick gloves are designed to help ranchers hold on to the squirming, slimy bodies of grindlegrubs, and help soothe the wear and tear of the day while restoring energy for after-hours pursuits. While wearing the gloves, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks. The gripping power of the gloves provides you with a +2 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves to [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=95). If you roll a success to Grab an Edge, you get a critical success instead, as the _gripping gloves_ allow you to hold on even with just a few fingertips.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You rub your hands together and take a deep breath to prepare for the rest of your day. You regain 3d8+10 Hit Points and feel refreshed, losing the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition. As normal for effects that remove fatigue, this doesn't remove any underlying source of fatigue, such as lack of sleep, causing the condition to return if the underlying source isn't addressed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Grub Gloves (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 87

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Grub Gloves (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 87

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, the circumstance bonus is +3, and you instead regain 6d8+20 Hit Points.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Grub Gloves (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 87

**Price** 22,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, the circumstance bonus is +4, and you instead regain 8d8+30 Hit Points.
name: Grub Gloves

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Grub Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2561)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Danila Kenn and Depa Stepwell of Common Goods developed these gloves after complaints from an exhausted grindlegrub rancher. Since their development, the gloves have become popular not just among grindlegrub ranchers, but laborers of all kinds in Stonebreach. The thick gloves are designed to help ranchers hold on to the squirming, slimy bodies of grindlegrubs, and help soothe the wear and tear of the day while restoring energy for after-hours pursuits. While wearing the gloves, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks. The gripping power of the gloves provides you with a +2 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves to [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=95). If you roll a success to Grab an Edge, you get a critical success instead, as the _gripping gloves_ allow you to hold on even with just a few fingertips.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You rub your hands together and take a deep breath to prepare for the rest of your day. You regain 3d8+10 Hit Points and feel refreshed, losing the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition. As normal for effects that remove fatigue, this doesn't remove any underlying source of fatigue, such as lack of sleep, causing the condition to return if the underlying source isn't addressed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnet Coin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2562)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 87

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Thera Heartslip commissioned these magical coins during her early thievery days and still has a number on hand to share with her confidants. This coin resembles an ordinary coin, but it's warm and seems to thrum slightly when touched. The coin's face features a very small fragment from the pressed portion that can be removed, such as a small star or one eye from the head on the face. Removing this fragment causes the coin to stop thrumming and feeling warm to the touch. Noticing the removed piece requires close inspection and a successful DC 19 Perception check.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are holding the coin fragment; **Effect** You focus on the rest of the coin, imagining its complete form in your hand. The rest of the coin teleports into your hand, reforming the complete coin. If it was near any other coins, it also teleports up to 3d10 coins with it into your hand. The coin can only teleport up to 15 miles; if you are farther when recalling the coin, it teleports the maximum distance toward you and lands in an open space.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Magnet Coin

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unexceptional](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2563)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 87

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to an item of light Bulk or less that is no larger than 5 feet by 5 feet by 5 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Merchants traveling into Stonebreach sometimes apply this accessory rune to specific wares to avoid the notice of thieves. Conversely, many thieves in Highhelm apply these runes to stolen goods to help them smuggle items out of the city. The item gains the effects of [_magic aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=178) to appear as non-magical. The rune's effects also cause anyone who closely observes or holds the item to believe it is a mundane version of that type of item, such as believing a magical [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) to be an ordinary dagger, unless the creature succeeds at a DC 18 Will save. Creatures that succeed their save see that there is more to the item than meets the eye, but aren't immediately aware of the _magic aura_ effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Unexceptional

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Words of Wisdom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2564)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 87

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattoo

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Yrisette developed these unique tattoos after months of tinkering and experimentation. This tattoo is always of a saying that has meaning to the person who wears it. While the words are hidden within a larger pattern and are nearly impossible to discern at a glance, they lend gravitas and power to the wearer's words. When the tattoo is applied, choose whether the phrase is of threatening words ([Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)), persuasive words ([Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5)), or dramatic words ([Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12)).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You speak your meaningful phrase from your tattoo out loud, emboldening your words for 1 minute. During this time, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks and checks of the skill associated with your phrase. When you roll a critical failure on a Diplomacy check or the associated skill during this time, you get a failure instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

</traits>

</row>

</column>

---

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You become emboldened for 1 hour and the bonus is +3.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Highhelm Drill](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2566)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 112

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Not to be outdone by their cousins in Dongun Hold, the artificers of Highhelm have developed a handheld magical equivalent to their clockwork drilling constructs and vehicles. The device is still in the testing phases, but early versions have been released to fund more development. Appearing as an unassuming yellow box with two handles normally, when the command word is spoken, a spiraling drill made of force emerges from its top. An active _Highhelm drill_ can be used as an [improvised weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=224), dealing damage on a Strike as though it had been used on a surface for one round with no additional damage from other sources.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The force drill appears and begins turning, dealing 5 force damage per round to any material against which you hold the drill. The drill ignores the first 4 Hardness of any material it damages. The drill remains active for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Highhelm Drill (Mark I)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 112

**Price** 85 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 3

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Highhelm Drill (Mark II)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 112

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 3

</row>

</column>

---

The drill deals 10 force damage per round, ignores the first 8 Hardness, and remains active for 5 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Highhelm Drill (Mark III)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 112

**Price** 8,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 3

</row>

</column>

---

The drill deals 15 force damage per round, ignores the first 12 Hardness, and remains active for 10 minutes.
name: Highhelm Drill

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Highhelm Drill](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2566)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 112

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Not to be outdone by their cousins in Dongun Hold, the artificers of Highhelm have developed a handheld magical equivalent to their clockwork drilling constructs and vehicles. The device is still in the testing phases, but early versions have been released to fund more development. Appearing as an unassuming yellow box with two handles normally, when the command word is spoken, a spiraling drill made of force emerges from its top. An active _Highhelm drill_ can be used as an [improvised weapon](/Rules.aspx?ID=224), dealing damage on a Strike as though it had been used on a surface for one round with no additional damage from other sources.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The force drill appears and begins turning, dealing 5 force damage per round to any material against which you hold the drill. The drill ignores the first 4 Hardness of any material it damages. The drill remains active for 1 minute.

</column>

---

The drill deals 10 force damage per round, ignores the first 8 Hardness, and remains active for 5 minutes.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Highhelm Stronghold Plate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2567)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 112

**Price** 3,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Bastion Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=285) [bastion plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)_ is made almost entirely of stone plates. It looks almost unwearable and is in fact only wearable because of numerous minor enchantments for comfort and mobility. If you have [armor specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=211) with heavy armor, your resistance while wearing _Highhelm stronghold plate_ applies to both slashing and piercing damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The plating on your armor explodes off of your body, expands, and reshapes, transforming into a stone structure with the effects of [_wall of stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=365). While the armor is transformed in this way, you don't gain the benefits of wearing the armor. The armor remains transformed for up to 10 minutes or until you Dismiss the effect, at which point the wall of stone disappears and the armor returns to you, donning itself immediately.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of _wall of stone_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Highhelm Stronghold Plate

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Highhelm War Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2568)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 112

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Razor Disc](/Shields.aspx?ID=14)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This specialized [razor disc](/Shields.aspx?ID=14) (Hardness 11, HP 84, BT 42) was originally designed by the members of Clan Molgrade for use by Highhelm's soldiers. The integrated blades are etched with runes, making them a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ weapon.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You critically hit with the shield's integrated blades;  **Effect** The shield's blades grow and dig themselves into your foe. The creature becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) as it becomes impaled on the shield spikes. While you have a creature grabbed in this way, you can't [Raise the Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98), but the creature takes [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) every round equal to the number of damage dice. This persistent bleed can't end as long as the creature is impaled on the shield. You can Release the shield to leave it embedded in the creature. This ends the grab but doesn't end the persistent bleed damage. The creature can remove the shield and end the grab by attempting a check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79). This normally uses your [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) DC to Escape, but if you're no longer holding the shield, the Escape DC is 25.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Highhelm War Shield (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 112

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Highhelm War Shield (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 112

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 16, HP 124, BT 62, and an Escape DC of 31. The integrated blades are a _+2 greater striking_ weapon.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Highhelm War Shield (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 112

**Price** 55,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 19, HP 150, BT 75, and an Escape DC of 43. The integrated blades are a _+3 major striking_ weapon.
name: Highhelm War Shield

markdown:
**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 113 --- The crowning achievement of Highhelm's millennia of metallurgical advancements and engineering is the beautiful alloy called keep stone. Despite its name, no stone is used in the production of keep stone. Through the use of a highly guarded process that smelts together [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) and lead, Highhelm's greatest crafters were able to develop a material with an appearance that more closely resembles marble than metal. Keep stone is only slightly weaker than adamantine alone, but with the incredible ability to disrupt magic. Any spell or magical effect targeting raw keep stone must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the effect is lost. Keep stone's use in the construction of Torag's Shield is not only to stretch Highhelm's adamantine reserves further and protect the walls from magical assault, but also because of its protective effects on [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) magic on both Highhelm itself and its immediate vicinity. If the target of a divination spell is in the presence of a large quantity of keep stone, including a structure, the caster must also succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the spell is lost. ## Keep Stone

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Keep Stone Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2570)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 113

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk**  varies by armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Keep Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2569)

</column>

---

Keep stone armor is nearly impossible to acquire due to the fact that most of Highhelm's keep stone reserves are intended to help complete the Torag's Shield project. Additionally, the sheer amount of keep stone required to craft a suit of keep stone armor is prohibitive. For those who can acquire a set, there are few better protections against magic. While wearing keep stone armor, the item bonus it provides to AC and saving throws increases by 1 against spells, and [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) spells targeting you must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or fail.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Keep Stone Armor (High-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 113

**Price** 56,000 gp (+ 5,600 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 28,000 gp of keep stone + 2,800 gp per bulk.
name: Keep Stone Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Keep Stone Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2571)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 113

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Keep Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2569)

</column>

---

Much easier to come by than armor, a keep stone shield offers effective-enough protection against magic that they've become very popular among the upper echelons of dwarven society, particularly those on active duty. When you have a keep stone shield Raised, you gain its circumstance bonus to saving throws against spells that target you (as well as AC). Additionally, you can [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) against any spell damage, not just from physical attacks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 113

**Price** 13,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 11, HP 46, and BT 23.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 6,600 gp of keep stone.
name: Keep Stone Shield

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Keep Stone Weapon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2572)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 113

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by weapon

**Bulk**  varies by weapon
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Keep Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2569)

</column>

---

As a material with defensive properties, keep stone isn't particularly prized for weapons, but it does offer a particular niche. When used with abilities that allow you to use a Strike to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) spells or magical effects, such as the [Sunder Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1624) feat, the counteract check gains a +2 circumstance bonus. If the counteract check is tied to the Strike's result, add the bonus when calculating your counteract result, but not to the attack roll. Keep stone is treated as [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) for the purpose of overcoming resistances.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Keep Stone Weapon (High-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 113

**Price** 22,500 gp (+ 2,250 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 11,250 gp of keep stone + 1,125 gp per bulk.
name: Keep Stone Weapon

markdown:
**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 115 --- Dwarven tales state that the first set of these stones was created by a [dwarven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) lapidary for his [elven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2) sweetheart. The gem he chose was amber—to show his clan that a union between stone and leaf was possible. His family remained hesitant, despite the unmistakable love that the men shared. The tales then say that it was [Folgrit](/Deities.aspx?ID=87) who grew impatient and transformed the stones as a sign of her approval. Since then, more sets of these have been created, but doing so is an unsure process. Some of the stones are seemingly given their magical abilities at random, which most attribute as further blessings from Folgrit. Each set of _affinity stones_ typically comes as a pair of gems set into worn items such as hilts, necklaces, or rings, though some sets of affinity stones feature additional gems. The full set of stones must be invested together across multiple creatures, or else their magic fails to function. To invest these items, one person must be a dwarf and everyone must perform a one-hour ceremony that includes divulging their innermost feelings. Their love need not be romantic, but it must be genuine and deep. If they pass this test, they receive the following benefits.
  • You are always aware of each other and permanently gain the effects of the [_status_](/Spells.aspx?ID=308) spell.
  • Non-dwarves gain the benefits of a 1st- or 5th-level ancestry [feat with the dwarf trait](/Feats.aspx?Traits=54), and dwarves gain the benefits one 1st- or 5th-level ancestry feat with a trait corresponding to the ancestry of another person with which the affinity stones are invested. For example, if you share investment in the stones with a [halfling](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=5), you can take halfling ancestry feats. You can't use these feats to meet prerequisites, but the benefits of the feats otherwise function as if you had taken the feat.
  • You gradually take on some of the physical characteristics of your counterparts. An elf may begin to grow a beard, while their dwarven counterpart may develop pointed ears. These changes have no mechanical effect.
**Destruction** If one of the people who had invested in the _affinity stones_ commits an act of true and regretless betrayal against someone else invested in the _affinity stones_, all of the stones shatter to dust.
name: Affinity Stones

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 22"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blade of Fallen Stars](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2574)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Combination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=417" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 115

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Gun Sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=218)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+4 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greater frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296) [gun sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=218)_ is made from [high-grade cold siccatite](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1426). This gun sword's elegant, silvery blade is double-edged and etched with stars and constellations that move and mirror the night sky. The blade absorbs starlight, and prolonged exposure causes it to form a layer of glittering residue. Smiths in Dongun Hold forged this weapon by combining the cracked remains of a skymetal [arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=195) and the shards of an exceptional [elven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2) [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24). Some tales of the forging report that the sword was _Tear of Eventide_, a great blade forged out of starlight for the battle between [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5) and Aolar. Whether or not that was the case, dwarven curators who examined the shards simply noted that the blade's material was compatible with the cracked gun. After an extremely tense diplomatic brunch, the weapon is now regarded as a miracle of elven artistry and dwarven engineering, and a powerful symbol of allyship between their kingdoms.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You unfold the parrying hooks extending from the blade to form a bipod, which has the same benefits as a typical [tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1207). This negates the –2 penalty from [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=409) but must be reversed by a subsequent Interact action before the weapon can be repositioned or wielded in melee.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;    **Requirements** The _blade of fallen stars_ was exposed to starlight for at least one hour since the last time this ability was used; **Effect** The coating on the blade seeps into the engravings and is collected in the barrel. On your next ranged Strike, the weapon fires a glowing white projectile with a blazing blue tail that explodes on impact. In addition to the normal Strike damage, the projectile also deals 6d8 cold damage and creates a [_faerie fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=107) effect in a 5-foot emanation centered on the target.

**Destruction** Depriving the weapon of all light, natural or otherwise, for seven years, seven months, and seven days will cause it to crumble and erode irreparably

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blade of Fallen Stars

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aerial Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2575)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 74

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This blue cloak is surprisingly light for its length and seems to catch wind bursts in its tail, flying out behind the wearer while cushioning falls and easing jumps. The sides of the cloak are slightly weighted, making it easy to grab ahold of when it fills with air. This cloak grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) and a +1 item bonus to Acrobatics checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28) or [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=30).

**Activate—Fall Gently** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're [falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=401);  **Effect** The cloak catches the air and you grab onto its edges, utilizing the draft to guide you to safety. Treat your fall as 30 feet shorter and glide to a space of your choice at the bottom of your fall, which must be within 20 feet of where you would've landed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aerial Cloak

    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
