>
[Blight Breath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2577)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Bottled Breath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=499" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 74

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This foul-smelling bottle contains compressed, noxious fumes. After you inhale the odious gases of the blight breath, you gain resistance 10 to [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) for as long as you hold your breath. You can exhale the blight breath as a single action. The resulting spray of noxious fumes deals 10d6 poison damage to each creature in a 15-foot cone, with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blight Breath

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Extra Lung](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2578)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 74

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An extra lung is a waterproofed bladder of air worn in an underarm holster, connected to the wearer's nose by a long tube. You can use it as a source of air instead of breathing in the air around you. It can hold 5 rounds' worth of breathable air, and can be refilled if the _extra lung_ is left open for 10 minutes in an environment with suitable air.

 You can switch to breathing from the _extra lung_ at any time, without using an action. You can use air from the bladder following the rules for [holding your breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468), but you can speak without losing the air from the extra lung. When you lose air at the end of each of your turns, choose whether you use breath you're holding or air from the _extra lung_. Speaking causes you to lose breath you're holding but doesn't affect the air in the _extra lung_.

**Activate—Cough Up** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))  **Trigger** You breathe in an [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) or other inhaled affliction;  **Effect** You cough the poison or tainted air into your _extra lung_, immediately attempting a new save against the effect. The air inside your _extra lung_ becomes fouled, and you re-expose yourself to the inhaled affliction if you breathe it in. The _extra lung_ is cleansed of any poison it contains every day at dawn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Extra Lung

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fan of Soothing Winds](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2579)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 74

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _fan of soothing winds_ has six cloud-shaped glass beads on the bottom of the fan, one on each of the exposed ribs. One side of the beads is white, and the other is a dark, stormy gray. Once flipped, a bead stays on its newly exposed side for an entire day before resetting overnight.

**Activate—Healing Wind** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day per bead;   **Effect** You open your fan and turn a bead  of your choice. The fan casts a 3-action [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell with an area of a 30-foot cone instead of a 30-foot emanation; the save DC for an undead creature is 28. This spell gains the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) trait. The rank of the spell depends on which bead you're turning: the first two beads cast 4th-rank _heal_, the center two cast 3rd-rank _heal_, and the last two cast 2nd-rank _heal_.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Floating Tent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2580)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This diamond-shaped tent is designed for sleeping in planar environments without gravity, such as the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4). Weights are attached at each of its six points, carefully balanced against each other to prevent the tent from leaning too far in any one direction. The tent has an anchor, which can be used to moor the tent and prevent it from floating away from the object or location it is anchored to.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Floating Tent (Pup)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 75

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

A floating pup tent is large enough for one person and their supplies. It contains one hammock suspended in the center of the tent, large enough for a Medium or smaller creature to comfortably sleep, in addition to a net beneath the hammock where gear can be stored.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Floating Tent (Four-Person)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 75

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

A standard floating tent is large enough for four creatures and their supplies, containing four hammocks in two rows suspended in the center of the tent and large nets near the top and bottom of the tent for gear.
name: Floating Tent
